channel.title: Check on FsFlow
channel.description: Recent content in Check on FsFlow

item.title: Check.all
item.description:
Returns success when every check in the sequence succeeds.

let all (checks: seq<Check<'value>>) : Check<unit>

Remarks

Sequentially evaluates each check in the checks sequence. Stops at the first failure.

Parameters

  • checks: A sequence of checks.

Returns

A Check that succeeds only if all inputs succeed.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.and
item.description:
Returns success when both checks succeed.

let ``and`` (left: Check<'left>) (right: Check<'right>) : Check<unit>

Remarks

This is a logical “and” operation. It short-circuits: if left fails, right is not evaluated.

Parameters

  • left: The first check.
  • right: The second check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds only if both inputs succeed.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.any
item.description:
Returns success when at least one check in the sequence succeeds.

let any (checks: seq<Check<'value>>) : Check<unit>

Remarks

Sequentially evaluates each check in the checks sequence. Stops at the first success.

Parameters

  • checks: A sequence of checks.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if any input succeeds.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.blank
item.description:
Returns success when the string is blank.

let blank (str: string) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the string is blank.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.equal
item.description:
Returns success when the values are equal.

let equal (expected: 'a) (actual: 'a) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • expected: The expected value.
  • actual: The actual value.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the values are equal.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIf
item.description:
Returns success when the condition is false.

let failIf (cond: bool) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • cond: The boolean condition to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if cond is false.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfBlank
item.description:
Returns the string when it is blank.

let failIfBlank (str: string) : Check<string>

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check containing the blank string.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfEmpty
item.description:
Returns the sequence when it is not empty.

let failIfEmpty (coll: seq<'a>) : Check<seq<'a>>

Parameters

  • coll: The sequence to check.

Returns

A Check containing the non-empty sequence.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfEmptyStr
item.description:
Returns the string when it is null or empty.

let failIfEmptyStr (str: string) : Check<string>

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check containing the empty or null string.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are equal.

let failIfEqual (expected: 'a) (actual: 'a) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • expected: The expected value.
  • actual: The actual value.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the values are equal.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfNone
item.description:
Returns the value when the option is Some.

let failIfNone (opt: 'a option) : Check<'a>

Parameters

  • opt: The option to check.

Returns

A Check containing the value if present.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfNonEmptyStr
item.description:
Returns success when the string is null or empty.

let failIfNonEmptyStr (str: string) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the string is null or empty.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfNotBlank
item.description:
Returns success when the string is blank.

let failIfNotBlank (str: string) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the string is blank.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfNotEmpty
item.description:
Returns success when the sequence is empty.

let failIfNotEmpty (coll: seq<'a>) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • coll: The sequence to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the sequence is empty.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfNotEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are not equal.

let failIfNotEqual (expected: 'a) (actual: 'a) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • expected: The expected value.
  • actual: The actual value.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the values differ.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfNotNull
item.description:
Returns success when the value is null.

let failIfNotNull (value: 'a when 'a : null) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • value: The value to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the value is null.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfNull
item.description:
Returns the value when it is null.

let failIfNull (value: 'a when 'a : null) : Check<'a>

Parameters

  • value: The value to check.

Returns

A Check containing the null value.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfSome
item.description:
Returns success when the option is None.

let failIfSome (opt: 'a option) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • opt: The option to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the option is None.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfValueNone
item.description:
Returns the value when the value option is ValueSome.

let failIfValueNone (opt: 'a voption) : Check<'a>

Parameters

  • opt: The value option to check.

Returns

A Check containing the value if present.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.failIfValueSome
item.description:
Returns success when the value option is ValueNone.

let failIfValueSome (opt: 'a voption) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • opt: The value option to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the value option is ValueNone.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.fromPredicate
item.description:
Builds a check from a predicate while preserving the successful value.

let fromPredicate (predicate: 'value -> bool) (value: 'value) : Check<'value>

Parameters

  • predicate: A function of type 'value -> bool to test the value.
  • value: The value of type 'value to check.

Returns

A Check containing the value if the predicate succeeds.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.not
item.description:
Returns success when the supplied check fails.

let not (check: Check<'value>) : Check<unit>

Remarks

This is a logical “not” operation for checks. Note that it discards the success value and returns Unit on success.

Parameters

  • check: The source Check to invert.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the input fails.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.notBlank
item.description:
Returns the string when it is not blank.

let notBlank (str: string) : Check<string>

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check containing the non-blank string.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.notEmpty
item.description:
Returns the sequence when it is not empty.

let notEmpty (coll: seq<'a>) : Check<seq<'a>>

Parameters

  • coll: The sequence to check.

Returns

A Check containing the non-empty sequence.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.notEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are not equal.

let notEqual (expected: 'a) (actual: 'a) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • expected: The expected value.
  • actual: The actual value.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the values differ.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.notNull
item.description:
Returns the value when it is not null.

let notNull (value: 'a when 'a : null) : Check<'a>

Parameters

  • value: The value to check.

Returns

A Check containing the non-null value.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.okIf
item.description:
Returns success when the condition is true.

let okIf (cond: bool) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • cond: The boolean condition to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if cond is true.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.okIfBlank
item.description:
Returns success when the string is blank.

let okIfBlank (str: string) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the string is null, empty, or whitespace.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.okIfEmpty
item.description:
Returns success when the sequence is empty.

let okIfEmpty (coll: seq<'a>) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • coll: The sequence to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the sequence is empty.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.okIfEmptyStr
item.description:
Returns success when the string is null or empty.

let okIfEmptyStr (str: string) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • str: The string to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the string is null or empty.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.okIfEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are equal.

let okIfEqual (expected: 'a) (actual: 'a) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • expected: The expected value.
  • actual: The actual value.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the values are equal.

Information

  • Module: Check

item.description:
Returns success when the option is None.

let okIfNone (opt: 'a option) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • opt: The option to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the option is None.

Information

  • Module: Check

item.description:
Returns the string when it is not null or empty.

let okIfNonEmptyStr (str: string) : Check<string>

Parameters

A Check containing the non-empty string.

A Check containing the non-blank string.

  • Module: Check

item.title: Check.okIfNotEmpty
item.description:
Returns the sequence when it is not empty.

let okIfNotEmpty (coll: seq<'a>) : Check<seq<'a>>

Parameters

  • coll: The sequence of type seq<'a> to check.

Returns

A Check containing the non-empty sequence.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.okIfNotEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are not equal.

let okIfNotEqual (expected: 'a) (actual: 'a) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • expected: The expected value.
  • actual: The actual value.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the values differ.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.okIfNotNull
item.description:
Returns the value when it is not null.

let okIfNotNull (value: 'a when 'a : null) : Check<'a>

Parameters

  • value: The value of type 'a to check for null.

Returns

A Check containing the non-null value.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.okIfNull
item.description:
Returns success when the value is null.

let okIfNull (value: 'a when 'a : null) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • value: The value to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the value is null.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.okIfSome
item.description:
Returns the value when the option is Some.

let okIfSome (opt: 'a option) : Check<'a>

Parameters

  • opt: The FSharpOption to check.

Returns

A Check containing the value if present.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.okIfValueNone
item.description:
Returns success when the value option is ValueNone.

let okIfValueNone (opt: 'a voption) : Check<unit>

Parameters

  • opt: The value option to check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if the value option is ValueNone.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.okIfValueSome
item.description:
Returns the value when the value option is ValueSome.

let okIfValueSome (opt: 'a voption) : Check<'a>

Parameters

  • opt: The FSharpValueOption to check.

Returns

A Check containing the value if present.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.or
item.description:
Returns success when either check succeeds.

let ``or`` (left: Check<'left>) (right: Check<'right>) : Check<unit>

Remarks

This is a logical “or” operation. It short-circuits: if left succeeds, right is not evaluated.

Parameters

  • left: The first check.
  • right: The second check.

Returns

A Check that succeeds if either input succeeds.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.orError
item.description:
Maps a unit error into the supplied application error value.

let orError (error: 'error) (result: Check<'value>) : Result<'value, 'error>

Remarks

This is the primary bridge from checks to domain-specific results.

Parameters

  • error: The domain error of type 'error to return on failure.
  • result: The source Check.

Returns

A Result with the provided error value.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source

item.title: Check.orErrorWith
item.description:
Maps a unit error into an application error produced on demand.

let orErrorWith (errorFn: unit -> 'error) (result: Check<'value>) : Result<'value, 'error>

Parameters

  • errorFn: A function of type unit -> 'error to produce the error.
  • result: The source Check.

Returns

A Result with the produced error value.

Information

  • Module: Check
  • Source: source
